channel.title: Posts on R1V3R5: An imaginary journal about fake rivers
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on R1V3R5: An imaginary journal about fake rivers

item.title: Hyrulean Hydrology: The riddle of Lake Hylia in 'Zelda: A Link to the Past'
item.description:
Introduction #

In The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past (ALttP), Lake Hylia receives water from Zora’s River. However, there exists no obvious outlfow![1]Zelda Wiki (2026). Lake Hylia This setup is peculiar and raises two linked (haha) questions: (1) what process keeps water level approximately stable, and (2) what process prevents long-run dissolved-load accumulation[2]Brutsaert, Wilfried (2005). Hydrology: An Introduction.

Our contribution examines the following hypotheses as candidate explanations the phenomenon:

Initial draft published on 2026-04-17.

No factual corrections have been recorded yet. Open an issue in the repository for map or terminology corrections.

No factual corrections have been made as of 2026-04-14.

If you spot an issue, please open a ticket in the project repository.
